semanticClasses.delete-now: No current runtime or entrypoint depends on the surface; delete it and keep stale-surface scans.
semanticClasses.migrate-now: Current code still touches an old or deprecated surface; migrate callers to the canonical contract and remove the old path.
semanticClasses.current-bridge: A bridge is still part of a current feature path; preserve only with owner, replacement, remove condition, and tests.
semanticClasses.runtime-resilience: Fallback handles current provider, model, GPU, media, network, file, cancellation, timeout, or capability absence.
semanticClasses.boundary-canonicalizer: Compatibility is isolated at an input boundary that converts multiple current inputs into one canonical shape.
semanticClasses.presentation-default: The word fallback describes UI copy, empty state, default dimensions, or placeholders rather than runtime failure handling.
semanticClasses.domain-status: The word deprecated is a current domain state such as market/entity deprecation, not a compatibility branch.
semanticClasses.generated-source: Generated output contains legacy/deprecated names; cleanup must update source schema/IDL and regenerated artifacts.
semanticClasses.test-only: The surface appears only in tests that protect current migration, guard, or deletion behavior.
semanticClasses.false-positive-word: The word appears in static-analysis notes or unrelated text and is not a code debt surface.

entries.surface: Storyboard table pre-schema sections reader and legacy media-ref normalization
entries.replacement: Canonical storyboard tables using scenes[] / shots[] and canonical media references.
entries.removeCondition: Removed in cleanup-remaining-legacy-debt-surfaces; canonical normalizer rejects pre-schema template/sections payloads.

entries.surface: Asset manifest legacy asset type migration and deprecated manifest aliases
entries.replacement: Canonical manifest package and asset type fields.
entries.removeCondition: Removed in cleanup-remaining-legacy-debt-surfaces; installed registry now accepts only canonical AssetType records and drops pre-v4 package type records.

sunsetProviders.currentPath: StorySymbolProjectSearchAdapter reads Fountain files through VSCode host APIs and the retained host-neutral @neko/content parser.
sunsetProviders.replacement: A Content/Fountain owner registers a ProjectSearchAdapter/provider with neko-search host integration.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Fountain partition tests pass without StorySymbolProjectSearchAdapter in compatAdapters.ts.

sunsetProviders.currentPath: AssetLibraryProjectSearchAdapter, MediaLibraryProjectSearchAdapter, and GeneratedAssetProjectSearchAdapter read current project cache/fact files through host JSON reader.
sunsetProviders.replacement: neko-assets and generated-asset owner register ProjectSearchAdapter providers or a neutral cache provider API.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: Asset, media, document, character-asset dimension, and generated asset search tests pass without compatAdapters.ts default adapters.

sunsetProviders.currentPath: Agent wraps the compat creative-entities adapter with neko-entity confirmed/context candidate providers.
sunsetProviders.replacement: neko-entity provider registration covers confirmed entities, candidates, requirements, and freshness aggregation.
sunsetProviders.removalTrigger: createAgentProjectSearchAdapters() no longer consumes createCompatibilityProjectSearchAdapters() for creative-entities.

entries.notes: This file is not an unused shim: it is still on the current Agent project search call path through @neko/search/host-vscode. Agent call-site ownership is tracked in the Agent LCD register; this non-Agent ledger entry covers the neko-search bridge itself.

entries.surface: Explicit local workspace metadata inspection and migration boundaries
entries.replacement: Canonical local metadata stores populated by explicit, verified migration commands that preserve valuable prelaunch workspace data.
entries.removeCondition: Remove individual migration readers only after retained workspaces no longer require the source format and inspection reports prove the format is absent or safely rebuildable.
entries.notes: These modules are explicit migration and inspection entrypoints, not successful runtime fallback readers. Verification failures throw and remain fail-visible.

entries.notes: Legacy prompt fields never become canonical authority implicitly; ambiguous or malformed inputs produce explicit diagnostics.

entries.notes: Canonical package loading does not read legacy metadata implicitly; the retained surface exists for explicit migration and diagnostics.

entries.notes: displayFallback selects the generic-table review presentation and never invokes an old runtime path or compatibility reader.
